title: fraxinus michauxii
intro: Fraxinus michauxii, also known as the Michaux's ash or green ash, is a deciduous tree species native to the southeastern United States. The bark of Fraxinus michauxii contains a compound called salicin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it useful for medicinal purposes. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus michauxii have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, with its health benefits including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as an antiseptic. Fraxinus michauxii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
